name: El Bierzo – circular from Ponferrada through Cabañas Raras, Canedo and Cacabelos
name_original: El Bierzo – circular desde Ponferrada por Cabañas Raras, Canedo y Cacabelos
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Ponferrada are set within the El Bierzo region, characterized by a natural basin surrounded by mountains up to 2,000 meters high. The landscape features a blend of thickening vineyards, extensive forests, and the confluence of the Sil and Boeza rivers. This diverse terrain offers varied elevation profiles, from river valleys to challenging mountain ascents, providing a rich environment for cycling exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Ponferrada The most popular touring cycling route is Montes de León – circular from Ponferrada to Montes de Valdueza and Peñalba de Santiago, a 31.4 miles (50.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 51 minutes to complete. This challenging route leads through mountainous terrain and historic villages. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ponferrada and Las Médulas Loop via Montes Aquilianos, a difficult 36.4 miles (58.5 km) path. This demanding route traverses unique Roman gold mining landscapes and mountain ranges. Local touring cyclists also love the El Bierzo – circular from Ponferrada through Cabañas Raras, Canedo and Cacabelos, a 31.1 miles (50.0 km) trail leading through the El Bierzo region, often completed in about 3 hours 10 minutes. Touring cycling around Ponferrada is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and historical sites like Las Médulas. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides through vineyards to difficult ascents in the Montes de León. The routes in Ponferrada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 1800 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ponferrada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of touring cycling routes around Ponferrada?
A: Ponferrada offers a diverse range of touring cycling routes. You'll find 8 easy routes, 47 moderate routes, and 69 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels. The terrain varies from river valleys to challenging mountain ascents in areas like the Montes de León.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Ponferrada?
A: Yes, there are 8 easy touring cycling routes available in Ponferrada. These routes often follow river valleys or pass through flatter vineyard areas, providing a gentler introduction to cycling in the region. While specific easy routes are not listed in the top recommendations, komoot offers options suitable for those new to touring cycling.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Ponferrada?
A: Absolutely. Ponferrada is an excellent destination for experienced touring cyclists, with 69 difficult routes available. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and traverse mountainous terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10503870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cebreiro Pass – Ascent to O Cebreiro loop from Ponferrada&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 99.2 km route with over 1400 meters of elevation gain, offering a true test of endurance.

Q: Can I cycle part of the Camino de Santiago from Ponferrada?
A: Yes, Ponferrada is a significant staging post on the Camino de Santiago (French Way). Many cyclists choose to ride the last 200 km from Ponferrada to Santiago de Compostela. This allows you to experience the rich cultural and natural highlights of the El Bierzo region and the picturesque O Cebreiro mountains, and even qualify for the Compostela certificate.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Ponferrada?
A: Many touring cycling routes around Ponferrada are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31542467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molinaseca – Cruz de Ferro (Iron Cross) loop from Ponferrada&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 53.2 km and takes you through significant historical points.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural features I can explore on a touring bike?
A: The region around Ponferrada is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the unique 'Martian landscape' of Las Médulas, a UNESCO World Heritage site resulting from Roman gold mining. The Montes de León offer stunning vistas, and you can find specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5190249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Montes de León&lt;/a&gt;. The confluence of the Sil and Boeza rivers also provides scenic riverside trails.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Ponferrada?
A: The El Bierzo region, where Ponferrada is located, benefits from a milder climate due to its natural basin surrounded by mountains. Spring (April-June) and Autumn (September-October) are generally ideal, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful landscapes without the intense heat of summer or the colder conditions of winter.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that pass through historical sites or villages?
A: Yes, Ponferrada's routes often combine natural beauty with rich history. You can cycle past the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/716062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Ferro (Iron Cross)&lt;/a&gt;, a significant landmark on the Camino de Santiago. Routes also lead through charming villages like Molinaseca, known for its medieval character, and Peñalba de Santiago, a serene mountain village with a 10th-century Mozarabic church.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Ponferrada?
A: The touring cycling routes in Ponferrada are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, which includes everything from river valleys and vineyards to challenging mountain ascents, as well as the opportunity to explore historical sites and the unique landscapes of El Bierzo.

Q: Are there any caves or interesting geological features to visit near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area around Ponferrada features several interesting caves and geological formations. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3444048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellán Galleries&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the Las Médulas mining landscape. Other notable caves include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3444033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Encantada Cave and La Cuevona&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3444083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Reirigo Cave&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3781415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Genadio Cave&lt;/a&gt;, offering unique natural and historical insights.

Q: Are there any mountain passes that can be tackled by touring cyclists in the region?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain surrounding Ponferrada includes several passes that are popular with touring cyclists. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3497179" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Morredero Pass&lt;/a&gt; is one such challenging ascent. Additionally, the region's proximity to the Montes de León and the Picos de Europa offers access to some spectacular mountain landscapes and passes for those seeking significant climbs.
tips: The castle consists of a large polygonal enclosure (more than 8,000 square meters) with double and triple defenses forming barbicans, towers, rooms and a large patio or albacar.

The Plaza del Ayuntamiento de Ponferrada is full of life at any time of the year.
With its multiple terraces, bars and restaurants, it is a meeting point for people of all ages.
The town hall building presides over the square.
In the entrance street you will also see the Clock Tower on the Arco de las Eras.

Road next to the reservoir that also borders the Compostilla II Thermal Power Plant. This plant was fueled by coal and was cooled by the water from the reservoir. It was closed and disconnected from the electricity grid in June 2020 due to its high polluting impact.
Sanctuary of the Fifth Anguish. This colossal temple, with a single nave, dates from the 18th century and is located where there was already a hermitage in the 12th century.

waypoints.name: View of the Bárcena Reservoir and the Compostilla II power plant
